title: rate limiter uses X-Forwarded-For without a trusted-proxy allowlist
description: In server.rs handle_inbox(), the client IP for rate limiting is taken from X-Real-IP or X-Forwarded-For headers with no validation that the request arrived through a trusted reverse proxy. When broadside is deployed directly on the internet (no proxy), any sender can spoof these headers to any value, making the rate limiter trivially bypassable: an attacker sends X-Real-IP: 1.2.3.4 to use a different bucket each request. The code has a comment warning about this, but the warning is only in source — nothing in the config or startup log tells the operator they are exposed.
close_reason: Accepted risk: documented as ponytail ceiling. Single-operator deployment behind reverse proxy.
